questions.question_text:
Choose the right answer.
Mr Willy Wonka is (a) a cook, (b) an inventor, (c) a manager.
Wonka-Vite makes people (a) older, (b) younger.
Mr Wonka wants to invent a new thing which will make people (a) younger, (b) older.
questions.question_html:
Choose the right answer.

  1. Mr Willy Wonka is (a) a cook, (b) an inventor, (c) a manager.
  2. Wonka-Vite makes people (a) older, (b) younger.
  3. Mr Wonka wants to invent a new thing which will make people (a) younger, (b) older.

questions.question_text: Can anyone's age be a minus number? What does minus 87 mean?
questions.question_html: Can anyone’s age be a minus number? What does minus 87 mean?

solutions.text: No, age cannot be in minus because we all start growing up from the moment we are born. 'Minus 87' means the man is 87 years back to his actual age.
solutions.html: No, age cannot be in minus because we all start growing up from the moment we are born. 'Minus 87' means the man is 87 years back to his actual age.

questions.question_text:
Mr. Wonka begins by asking himself two questions. What are they?
What is ________?
What lives ________?
questions.question_html:
Mr. Wonka begins by asking himself two questions. What are they?

  1. What is ________?
  2. What lives ________?

solutions.text:
What is the oldest thing in the world?
What lives longer than anything else?
solutions.html:
1. What is the oldest thing in the world?
  2. What lives longer than anything else?

questions.question_text: What trees does Mr. Wonka mention? Which tree does he say lives the longest?
questions.question_html: What trees does Mr. Wonka mention? Which tree does he say lives the longest?

solutions.text: The trees that Mr. Wonka mentions are the Douglas fir, the oak, the cedar, and the Bristlecone pine. He says that the Bristlecone pine lives the longest.
solutions.html: The trees that Mr. Wonka mentions are the Douglas fir, the oak, the cedar, and the Bristlecone pine. He says that the Bristlecone pine lives the longest.

questions.question_text: How long does this tree live? Where can you find it?
questions.question_html: How long does this tree live? Where can you find it?

solutions.text: Bristlecone pine tree lives for over 4000 years. It can be found upon the slopes of Wheeler Peak in Nevada, U.S.A.
solutions.html: Bristlecone pine tree lives for over 4000 years. It can be found upon the slopes of Wheeler Peak in Nevada, U.S.A.

questions.question_text: How many of the oldest living things can you remember from Mr. Wonka's list? (Don't look back at the story!) Do you think all these things really exist, or are some of them purely imaginary?
questions.question_html: How many of the oldest living things can you remember from Mr. Wonka’s list? (Don’t look back at the story!) Do you think all these things really exist, or are some of them purely imaginary?

solutions.text:
Mr. Wonka has mentioned some very oldest things. He said he had collected a pint of sap from a 4000 years Bristlecone pine tree, toe-nail clippings from a 168 years old Russian Farmer, an egg laid by a 200 years old tortoise, the tail of 51 years old horse, whiskers of a 36 years old cat called Crumpets, a flea that lived on Crumpets for 36 years, tail of a 207 years old rat, the black teeth of a 97 years old Grimalkin and knucklebones of a 700 years old Catalog.
Very few of these things would have really existed, a majority of them are purely imaginary.
solutions.html:
Mr. Wonka has mentioned some very oldest things. He said he had collected a pint of sap from a 4000 years Bristlecone pine tree, toe-nail clippings from a 168 years old Russian Farmer, an egg laid by a 200 years old tortoise, the tail of 51 years old horse, whiskers of a 36 years old cat called Crumpets, a flea that lived on Crumpets for 36 years, tail of a 207 years old rat, the black teeth of a 97 years old Grimalkin and knucklebones of a 700 years old Catalog.
Very few of these things would have really existed, a majority of them are purely imaginary.

questions.question_text: Why does Mr. Wonka collect items from the oldest things? Do you think this is the right way to begin his invention?
questions.question_html: Why does Mr. Wonka collect items from the oldest things? Do you think this is the right way to begin his invention?

solutions.text: Mr. Wonka collected items from the oldest living things because he wanted to create age; he wanted to invent something that would make people older. It was probably the right way to begin his invention as he wanted to make people older and therefore, the use of old things seems appropriate.
solutions.html: Mr. Wonka collected items from the oldest living things because he wanted to create age; he wanted to invent something that would make people older. It was probably the right way to begin his invention as he wanted to make people older and therefore, the use of old things seems appropriate.

questions.question_text: What happens to the volunteer who swallows four drops of the new invention? What is the name of the invention? [The Invention of Vita Wonk]
questions.question_html: What happens to the volunteer who swallows four drops of the new invention? What is the name of the invention? [The Invention of Vita Wonk]

solutions.text: The moment the volunteer swallows four drops of the new invention, he begins wrinkling and shrivelling up all over. His hair starts dropping off and his teeth start falling out. Before Mr Wonka knew it, the volunteer suddenly becomes an old man of seventy-five. The name of the invention was Vita-Wonk.
solutions.html: The moment the volunteer swallows four drops of the new invention, he begins wrinkling and shrivelling up all over. His hair starts dropping off and his teeth start falling out. Before Mr Wonka knew it, the volunteer suddenly becomes an old man of seventy-five. The name of the invention was Vita-Wonk.

questions.question_text:
Fill in the blanks in the recipe given below with words from the box.


shred, cooker, times, tomatoes, half, onion, oil


Easy Palak-Dal
INGREDIENTS
One ________
One cup dal
Two thin green chillies
________a teaspoon red chilli powder
Eight small bunches of palak
Two
Salt to taste
Wash and cut the vegetables; ________ the palak. Put everything in a pressure ________. Let the cooker whistle three ________, then switch it off. Fry a few cumin seeds in ________ and add to the palak-dal.
questions.question_html:
Fill in the blanks in the recipe given below with words from the box.

shred, cooker, times, tomatoes, half, onion, oil

Easy Palak–Dal
INGREDIENTS

  • One ________
  • One cup dal
  • Two thin green chillies
  • ________a teaspoon red chilli powder
  • Eight small bunches of palak
  • Two
  • Salt to taste

Wash and cut the vegetables; ________ the palak. Put everything in a pressure ________. Let the cooker whistle three ________, then switch it off. Fry a few cumin seeds in ________ and add to the palak–dal.

solutions.text:
INGREDIENTS
One onion
One cup dal
Two thin green chillies
Half a teaspoon red chilli powder
Eight small bunches of palak
Two tomatoes
Salt to taste
Wash and cut the vegetables; shred the palak. Put everything in a pressure cooker. Let the cooker whistle three times, then switch it off. Fry a few cumin seeds in oil and add to the palak-dal.
solutions.html:
INGREDIENTS

  • One onion
  • One cup dal
  • Two thin green chillies
  • Half a teaspoon red chilli powder
  • Eight small bunches of palak
  • Two tomatoes
  • Salt to taste

Wash and cut the vegetables; shred the palak. Put everything in a pressure cooker. Let the cooker whistle three times, then switch it off. Fry a few cumin seeds in oil and add to the palak–dal.

questions.question_text: A family tree is a diagram that shows the relationship between the different members of a family. Fill in the family tree below with names, ages, and other details you think are relevant (you may even stick photographs if you have them). Put your family trees up in the class.
questions.question_html: A family tree is a diagram that shows the relationship between the different members of a family. Fill in the family tree below with names, ages, and other details you think are relevant (you may even stick photographs if you have them). Put your family trees up in the class.

questions.question_text:
Make a list of the trees Mr. Wonka mentions. Where do these trees grow? Try to find out from an encyclopedia. Write a short paragraph about two or three of these trees.
Name some large trees commonly found in your area. Find out something about them (How old are they? Who planted them? Do birds eat their fruit?), and write two or three sentences about each one of them.
questions.question_html:
1. Make a list of the trees Mr. Wonka mentions. Where do these trees grow? Try to find out from an encyclopedia. Write a short paragraph about two or three of these trees.
  2. Name some large trees commonly found in your area. Find out something about them (How old are they? Who planted them? Do birds eat their fruit?), and write two or three sentences about each one of them.

solutions.text:
Mr. Wonka mentioned the name of the following trees:
Douglas Fir, Oak, Cedar, Bristlecone Pine
Douglas Fir is a native tree of Western North America. Its scientific name is Pseudotsuga Menziesii. It is also called Oregon pine or Douglas spruce. It is named in honour of David Douglas. He was a Scottish botanist and a collector and was the first one to report about the nature and potential of the species.
Oak trees are native to the Northern hemisphere and include deciduous and evergreen species. It is found in abundance in Northern America with over 90 species found in the United States and 160 in Mexico. Oakwood has a wide application because of its resistance to fungal and insect attack.
Cedar trees are found in abundance all across the world. Cedarwoods have a wide application like used to make pencils. Australian red cedar is highly valued and is used for making furniture and shipbuilding.
Bristlecone pine trees have some species that are more than 5000 years old and are the oldest known individuals of any species. The tree got its name from the prickles on the female cones. They are found in abundance in the United States of America.
Some of the commonly found trees in our area include eucalyptus, mango, neem, peepal, and banyan trees. Eucalyptus trees are very tall in structure. They have medicinal properties and are available in large numbers in our area.
Mango trees are generally planted in houses for their use in religious purposes. It is also planted in large numbers for the fruit.
Neem is found in houses as well as parks. They have medicinal properties and are widely scattered all across our area.
Peepal and Banyan trees are mostly found in temples. They are worshipped by Hindus. Some of these trees are over 100 years old.
solutions.html:
1. Mr. Wonka mentioned the name of the following trees:
    Douglas Fir, Oak, Cedar, Bristlecone Pine
    Douglas Fir is a native tree of Western North America. Its scientific name is Pseudotsuga Menziesii. It is also called Oregon pine or Douglas spruce. It is named in honour of David Douglas. He was a Scottish botanist and a collector and was the first one to report about the nature and potential of the species.
    Oak trees are native to the Northern hemisphere and include deciduous and evergreen species. It is found in abundance in Northern America with over 90 species found in the United States and 160 in Mexico. Oakwood has a wide application because of its resistance to fungal and insect attack.
    Cedar trees are found in abundance all across the world. Cedarwoods have a wide application like used to make pencils. Australian red cedar is highly valued and is used for making furniture and shipbuilding.
    Bristlecone pine trees have some species that are more than 5000 years old and are the oldest known individuals of any species. The tree got its name from the prickles on the female cones. They are found in abundance in the United States of America.
  2. Some of the commonly found trees in our area include eucalyptus, mango, neem, peepal, and banyan trees. Eucalyptus trees are very tall in structure. They have medicinal properties and are available in large numbers in our area.
    Mango trees are generally planted in houses for their use in religious purposes. It is also planted in large numbers for the fruit.
    Neem is found in houses as well as parks. They have medicinal properties and are widely scattered all across our area.
    Peepal and Banyan trees are mostly found in temples. They are worshipped by Hindus. Some of these trees are over 100 years old.

questions.question_text:
Find out something interesting about age, or growing old, and write a paragraph about it. Following are a few topics, suggested as examples:
The age profile of a country's population - does it have more young people, than old people or vice versa? What are the consequences of this?
How can we tell how old a tree, a horse, or rock is?
What is the 'life expectancy' of various living things, and various populations (how long can they reasonably expect to live)?
questions.question_html:
Find out something interesting about age, or growing old, and write a paragraph about it. Following are a few topics, suggested as examples:

  • The age profile of a country’s population — does it have more young people, than old people or vice versa? What are the consequences of this?
  • How can we tell how old a tree, a horse, or rock is?
  • What is the ‘life expectancy’ of various living things, and various populations (how long can they reasonably expect to live)?

solutions.text:
All living beings start ageing from the moment they are born. The best part of the process of growing old is that we are able to witness many changes. India is one of the most populous nations in the world. In India, we have more of a youth population than old people. It increases productivity and earning capacity in society.
We can guess the age of a tree by counting the rings in its trunk. The age of rock and horse can be identified by the carbon dating process. Micro-organisms have the shortest life cycle.
Insects have a short life. Animals like elephant and tortoise live for more than 100 years. The life expectancy of a human is around 85 years.
solutions.html:
• All living beings start ageing from the moment they are born. The best part of the process of growing old is that we are able to witness many changes. India is one of the most populous nations in the world. In India, we have more of a youth population than old people. It increases productivity and earning capacity in society.
  • We can guess the age of a tree by counting the rings in its trunk. The age of rock and horse can be identified by the carbon dating process. Micro-organisms have the shortest life cycle.
  • Insects have a short life. Animals like elephant and tortoise live for more than 100 years. The life expectancy of a human is around 85 years.
